calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Continued To Nov-18-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Order Clarifying Prior Court Order Of December 19, 2024, Or, In The Alternative, Motion For An Order Staying The Entire Action Until Plaintiff And The Uberrelated Defendants Complete Binding Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Norman L. Pearl
Off Calendar Apr-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS RENEE FLORES AND VISUAL EDGE TECHNOLOGY, INC'S MOTION FOR ORDER CLARIFYING PRIOR COURT ORDER OF DECEMBER 19, 2024, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR AN ORDER STAYING THE ENTIRE ACTION UNTIL PLAINTIFF AND THE UBER RELATED DEFENDANTS COMPLETE BINDING ARBITRATION

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Renee Flores and Visual Edge Technology, Inc.'s motion for order clarifying prior court order of December 19, 2024 is granted. The order of December 19, 2024 stayed the entirety of this action until the completion of arbitration proceedings between plaintiff Abdelmajid Sabour and the Uber defendants. The Uber defendants' motion to compel arbitration and stay action contended that the arbitration agreements between them and Sabour provided that the "Federal Arbitration Act will govern" the "interpretation and enforcement" of the parties' arbitration agreements. (Uber Memo at 10:2-4). Unlike the California Arbitration Act which the parties correctly point out does not require stay of the entirety of a case when an issue therein is arbitrable, section 3 of the Federal Arbitration Act "provides that, when any issue in a suit is subject to arbitration, the court 'shall on application of one of the parties stay the trial of the action until such arbitration has been had...'" (Smith v. Spizzirri (2024) 601 US 472, 476). Thus, in accordance with the FAA's mandatory stay of the entirety of a case when an issue therein is determined to be arbitrable, the December 19, 2024 order requires the stay of the entirety of this case. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
